The views were breathtaking, and the scenery and waterfalls were to die for. However, the experience was made very uncomfortable by crowding: six people were packed into a helicopter designed for four. At a price of over $450 per person, this overcrowding felt misaligned with the value of the ride. The overall experience was extraord

reviews.author: Mauro Albrizio
reviews.text:
I am sorry to have to give you this but try to see it as an opportunity to improve.

A horrible experience unfortunately.

You really need to understand what kind of business you are running and that you customers are NOT just wallets.

This is an activity that for many can be extremely scary… yet very fascinating.
Any mind approaching this experience would suffer a bit of excitement as well as a bit of fear.

Today is extremely windy here. So the first question that a customer would ask is “wil

reviews.author: Melissa Diaz
reviews.text: Fantastic tour from start to finish! Staff were knowledgeable and friendly. Drew, our pilot, was amazing! His knowledge of the area and smooth flying made our experience perfect! He was great at making sure everyone had great views and photo opportunities! Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Martha Laning
reviews.text: We haven’t done a helicopter tour before and we picked Air Maui - without doors and it couldn’t have been better. Spectacular sights and Richard made the trip spectacular.  Smooth ride and great story telling. Loved it all.

reviews.author: Bobbijean Buster
reviews.text: The absolute best part of our trip, possibly the best thing either of us have ever experienced. We did get canceled twice due to weather but they made it right and it was worth the wait! We did the Doors Off Molokai and had the front seats. So worth it. The pilot, Dylan had an amazing playlist that was perfectly timed with what we were seeing and had a lot of knowledge about the area. I think our jaws dropped the whole trip! Thank you Air Maui for your hospitality, customer service and an incred

reviews.author: Haifa A.
reviews.text: We had an amazing experience at Air Maui with the doors-off helicopter tour. The instructions given were clear and all safety measures were done carefully by the staff. The tour itself was outstanding, our pilot made sure we saw all the great landmarks from both sides of the craft. This experience is worth booking and I would definitely do it again with Air Maui.

reviews.author: Suz Abare
reviews.text: Great helicopter rides over West Maui! Coastlines and waterfalls and whales . . . oh my!!

reviews.author: Alyssa Drosnock
reviews.text: Must do! We did the doors off helicopter tour and it was worth every penny. Our pilot Drew narrated the whole trip and made some fun turns while making everyone feel safe. It was incredible.

reviews.author: Scott Donnelly
reviews.text: We took the 45 minute West Maui and Molikai tour with Maui Air.  Captain Kirk was our pilot and he was great; both entertaining and informative.  The scenery was beautiful and the tour was the highlight of our Hawaiian vacation.  Treat yourself to an Air Maui tour, you'll be glad you did!

reviews.author: Jen White
reviews.text: My husband had a great time! I booked him a middle seat last minute for $100! Great experience. These doors on and doors off rides. For his tour the doors were on but the doors are all glass. They recommend you wear dark clothing as to not cause a reflection in the glass. It makes for better photos for everyone. :) They took off from a section of the airport and went all over the West side of Maui. Hanging low passing waterfalls, the coastline, & exquisite jungle views. The ride is about an hour

reviews.author: Evg Vfv
reviews.text: They organized an unforgettable helicopter tour for a very reasonable price. I definitely recommend trusting these guys. They are professionals! I was especially impressed by the pilot, who told many interesting things and cracked a lot of jokes.

reviews.author: Jennifer Calvery
reviews.text: A perfect sunrise tour! Enjoyed our doors-on 45 min flight over the volcanoes, rainforest, and waterfalls without driving the road to Hana. Spectacular views and friendly skies.

reviews.author: bobspryn
reviews.text: A fun atmosphere, good food, great service!

reviews.author: MichiganTourists1
reviews.text: It was a great evening. Several locals recommended this as the best so we thought we’d give it a try. It’s a unique and memorable Hawaiian experience. Very happy we went.

reviews.author: C3066JXkathleenc
reviews.text: This Luau was incredible. The food was amazing, literally eleven single bite had so much flavor and taste. We were blown away. The venue is super cute and right on the water. The performers were amazing, it’s obvious that they’re passionate about hula. 10/10 experience.

reviews.author: samuelmJ3760IV
reviews.text: Time to try Drums of the Pacific instead. Gone here many times. Always bring guests new to Maui here. Tried very hard to be loyal to support Lahaina but enough is enough. Good things first: beautiful setting right by the beach. Parking across the street is confusing but available and free. Watch the sunset. Nice fresh flower leis. (Remember not to trash them.) Jewelry and crafts, but more sales than demonstrations, which annoyed our guests. (Some good prices though, i.e. on kukui necklaces, but

reviews.author: KaceeBOP
reviews.text: The staff here are great and the location is stunning. Food is good and not a buffet style, which I appreciated. Still lots of options and large portions. The entertainment started later and didn’t include any fire dancers - but felt like an authentic luau

reviews.author: F1367GKkathyw
reviews.text: Wonderful luau atmosphere, amazing food, and fantastic hula program. Best of all were the wait and bus staff. Friendliest and most congenial local men and women. Thankful this business is employing so many whose lives were impacted in the 2023 fires.

reviews.author: 689katyb
reviews.text: This had been on my radar for many years until I recently visited, and it still exceeded expectations!  Arrive with an empty stomach--there is so much food and all of it is delicious!  I would gladly go again if I am ever lucky enough to find myself in Maui again.

reviews.author: N3883OWcarolynl
reviews.text: This lu'ua was welcoming, beautifully presented and more. It was deeply connected to Hawaiian history, culture and traditions unlike other lu'uas which are more about flashy general presentations not specific to Hawaii. The food was also residential of Hawaiian ingredients plus being delicious. And I'm critical about that! But ordinarily I appreciated the narrative, the dancing and the music. Plus the overall presentation was captivating and meaningful. Leaving you with a much deeper connection

reviews.author: Linda D
reviews.text:
The Old Lahaina Luau was a major disappointment for our party of 6, plus a young child on January 17, 2026.
Having read recent rave reviews and having been to other islands’ luaus, we looked forward to an authentic experience for our first-time to Hawaii friends.
We must’ve hit a bad night because the food was mediocre and the show was low energy with the same 6-7 dancers in each scene. The host seemed to follow a script used for big tours, asking who had an anniversary, a birthday, newlyweds et

reviews.author: TLGElkGrove_CA
reviews.text: Have been to more than my fair share of luaus on Oahu and Maui thanks to three years at Pearl Harbor (with frequent visitors) and many trips post-Navy.  Old Lahaina Luau is far and away the best.  Very good food and drinks, exceptionally friendly staff, great pacing of the entire production and superb show, THIS is the luau Hawaii visitors should attend.  The productions at hotels are fine but pale in comparison and make this one well worth the extra cost, especially since none are cheap these d

reviews.author: Paula O
reviews.text: We were looking for a fun and culturally authentic luau- and the Old Lahaina Luau fit the bill.   From the moment we entered the parking lot, we were welcomed by friendly staff and directed to check in, which was extremely efficient.  Within 5 minutes we were at our table with a tropical drink in hand, enjoying the beautiful oceanfront location. Our server Kona was pleasant, fun, engaging, and explained so much about the food and cultural experience. She took great pride in her heritage and we w

reviews.author: DriveAllNight
reviews.text: Food was good - a nice assortment with a pre fixe menu. The show was well done; a nice explanation of Hawaiian history. Only complaint is that the performance faces one way for a good deal of it. If you’re fortunate to get seated on that side - you win!

reviews.author: Howrenrob
reviews.text: The staff here is very attentive from the time you arrived until the time you left.  You were met outside and helped to sign in.  Then met at the door to be escorted to your table with a drinkin hand.  Activities to keep you entertained while waiting for pig to be dug up and food to be served. Less activities then it was 9 years ago. Food had lots of variety and was very good. It is served to you at the table (no buffet lines) and you can ask for more if it is not enought or if you like a partic

reviews.author: V5227QQryank
reviews.text: Old Lahaina Luau was an amazing experience! The food was outstanding. It was extremely fresh and delicious. The service throughout the whole night was wonderful and attentive. The events and sunset before the dinner were special. The dancing was amazing and so much fun to watch. Completely worth it and it's a night we'll always remember!

reviews.author: NEguy77
reviews.text: Great evening near the water with great food and awesome performance! Its really neat to see some of the old Hawaiian traditions showcased. Highly recommended! Best mai tai on the island, too, by the way!

reviews.author: hazelnut71
reviews.text:
It was an amazing experience and vegetarian food was good too. Book early!

I would recommend getting there early so you can enjoy the Botanical Gardens and waterfalls. Will need a towel and swim shorts if you want to enjoy the falls.

Please, please have your own vehicle for transportation. After the show, we had to wait 2 hrs before we could get a Lyft. Also there is not good internet connection. This place is quite far from the city.

reviews.author: 240jma
reviews.text: Wonderful luau that we thoroughly enjoyed.  The food and drinks were really good, and we liked being seated at a reserved table and served.  The atmosphere and greeting were amazing; the storytelling and dancing was great. The price was a little high but at the end of the night we weren't disappointed.  One note:  no fire dancing at this luau.

reviews.author: SGIROX
reviews.text: I would love to give Old Lahaina Luau a 5-star review, because the setting, service, food, and show are all very good.   And - their banana bread is the best in the world by far.   However, I was disappointed in the business side of the event.   When we arrived, we were told to enjoy a complimentary drink in which the bartender said "the first one's one me".   After that, we had another round chosen from a drink menu with no prices.   At the end of the night, we found out that it is an open bar

reviews.author: lvdavelv
reviews.text: My family thoroughly enjoyed the evening at the Old Lahaina Luau. Service provided by Toko and Manny was excellent and provided everything we needed. Food was fantastic and had everything for everyone to enjoy. We absolutely plan to return on our next trip to Maui.

reviews.author: Kelly K
reviews.text: Perfect night. The setting was absolutely beautiful - both the venue and the sunset. Well organized. Drinks were flowing. They were not watered down despite other reviews. The food was amazing. There was so much of it.  It was the best ahi tuna I've ever had. The show was very nice and relaxing but they dont do a fire show which is kind of disappointing. The atmosphere was so lovely and relaxing. I would go again.

reviews.author: Matt Cloutier
reviews.text: This was our 3rd time here and we always come back. Thier show is amazing and learning about the history of Hawaii is fascinating. The food comes out in like 5 increments and each is so good. The main course is steak chicken and fish. Every point of this place is exceptional. Great for kids. They have activities set up before the show!

reviews.author: Bob Bobberson
reviews.text: We had a really great time here. The traditional hula was fantastic, and all of the food was exceptionally good. I really appreciate the way they bring out all courses rather than going with a buffet - it made us try things we probably wouldn't have, and we didn't have to wait in lines or compete with others. Just know that it can feel quite cold, even if its 70 degrees out, due to the wind (assuming you get a windy day). Most of our party was wearing light coats and cardigans and were still fre

reviews.author: Armin Engert
reviews.text:
Absolutely Magical Evening – The Best Luau in Maui!
We had the most wonderful time last night at the Old Lāhainā Lūʻau with my niece and her fiancé. It was truly a highlight of our time in Maui!
From the moment we arrived, the aloha spirit was in full effect. The VIP service was outstanding — we were seated at the best table with perfect views of the stage and the stunning oceanfront sunset. The staff made us feel like honored guests the entire night.
The food was incredible — fresh, flavorful H

reviews.author: Allison Beecher
reviews.text:
We booked this luau as an afterthought on our trip because we figured "you can't go to Hawaii without going to a luau." We were prepared for a really commercial lackluster "touristy" experience, and we could not have been more wrong. This ended up being one of our favorite experiences from our trip!

The atmosphere on the water was so gorgeous and romantic, especially once the sun went down and the torches lit the area. The food was delicious. The performers and the way the story was told was se

reviews.author: Greg C.
reviews.text:
My wife and I went on March 2nd as part of our first visit to Hawaii. We knew we should particpiate in a luau experience.

We enjoyed it a lot, everyone working there was smiling, friendly  and so kind. All great! We didn't like some of the food, but that's to be expected and not a complaint (I did try nearly everything, just to fully take in the experience).

All the food presentation was excellent, the server did a good job explaining what we were eating. Enjoyed the open bar service, that was

reviews.author: David Peterson
reviews.text: We have been to Old Lahaina Luau 3 times and have the best experience each time! Best staff, food, and dance!

reviews.author: Adolfo Ruiz
reviews.text: Great Luau experience. The service was first class and the entertainment did not disappoint.

reviews.author: linda chapa
reviews.text: This luau was amazing! Every detail was well thought out, different foods explained and the stories with the performances were beautiful. I highly recommend!

reviews.author: Madeline Umali
reviews.text:
We had such a great experience at Old Lahaina Luau—definitely one of the highlights of our time in Maui.

It felt very authentic, and the food was a standout. You get to try a wide variety of traditional Hawaiian dishes, which is perfect if you’re curious but don’t want to commit to a full entrée of something unfamiliar.

We chose the traditional seating on pillows at low tables, which was a fun and unique experience (though by hour two our backs were definitely feeling it—that one’s on us!).

T

reviews.author: A K
reviews.text: Gross mismanagement. While all the Luaus in town were canceling, these guys were confident they could pull it off. They kept telling that if there was a cancellation, they would let us know prior to the event, even if it was closer to the event. I even called 15 minutes prior to the event to confirm if they were canceling, and they said no, the show was going to continue. Eventually, after we all got drenched in the rain waiting, they told us around 6:10 p.m. that they were not moving forward an

reviews.author: Hayden Black
reviews.text: Rainy forecast for days. All signs point to rain showers in the evening, to begin one hour before event start. They don’t cancel. Everyone shows up and stands in the predicted rain for ~ 30 minutes. Event cancelled (should’ve been cancelled hours / days before people wasted their precious vacation hours on preparing / driving / waiting to come to your event.) “Thanks for coming out. Try rescheduling.” Next available opening? Next month. Okay, I’ll extend my vacation by four weeks just to come to

reviews.author: Peach Matthew
reviews.text:
If you are in Maui for vacation, this is absolutely a must for your entire group! The deep history and tradition that comes with the meal and experience is a memory you'll always carry. I come back time and time again, and always look forward to the magical sunsets, food and entertainment.

You reserve your table well in advance and I suggest you get a full table vs the traditional floor seat unless you group is young enough that the floor sounds fun.

The meal is served family style and they ke

reviews.author: Samantha Gutiérrez
reviews.text: It was wonderful.

reviews.author: Elena Denisova
reviews.text:
Manager Carrie was deeply disappointing and, frankly, unprofessional. Despite clearly having better tables available, we were placed in the very back at a shared table with a poor view. We approached her twice, as she suggested, but nothing was done—and she never once checked on us throughout the evening.
We had booked this experience several weeks in advance and specifically requested a nice table, as it was a very special anniversary for us.
For a $450 dinner, nothing felt special or romantic—

reviews.author: Jessica Tong
reviews.text: Great experience! Show was so cool!

reviews.author: Elias Patsalos
reviews.text: Place is great. Show is amazing , food is good though manager Carrie is absolutely unprofessional. We had an anniversary and did nothing to get us a better seating . Didn’t even come for once to check on us during the show. Unacceptable. She shouldn’t be there !

reviews.author: Albert Garza
reviews.text: Toko was a great server and makes us feel like family.

reviews.author: Andrea Seritt
reviews.text: One of the highlights of our trip!  My 2 kids LOVED the experience, the food, and the dancing!

reviews.author: 420henryj
reviews.text: The Chiefs Luau was a great evening.  The food was good and the show was great fun.  A talented group of women & men preserving a bit of island history and passing it on to the audience.  You are well feed and well treated. About a 30 minute ride from Waikiki.  It was well worth it.

reviews.author: Sofia B
reviews.text:
Worst costumer service experience ever.

If you are looking to waste an afternoon of your trip at a remote place far from Waikiki where their offices wait til the very last minute to help you out— this is your place. The incompetency alone of the people on the contact points is discouraging.

My husband and I (on our honeymoon) booked this Luau and checked three times with customer services to make sure they would not cancel the luau. At all times, they told us that the luau was not cancelled an

reviews.author: Discover54271009138
reviews.text: We wanted to go to a good luau. We did our research and decided upon cheap luau so glad we did had the best time and the best food I didn’t want the night to end. If we ever are fortunate enough to get back to Hawaii, we will definitely go to another Chiefs luau.

reviews.author: PeteS596
reviews.text:
I just  read the woman last review and I disagree. This was a great experience. Our transportation got us there a little late because of traffic, but nothing we could do. The food was ok nothing great but some good, there was plenty and you could go back and get as much as you want. The drinks were limited some beer and Mai Tai. And water Y
They sell shirts and your pictures 1 for 35 and 2 for 50. All that was average  but the show was fantastic real knives swords and fire dancers. The chief did

reviews.author: D2280ZBmaryp
reviews.text:
WORST Luau in Hawaii!!!!!
Food is HORRIBLE
Gregg the guy who checked us in was extremely rude. We were celebrating our daughters 50th birthday with 16 guests. They did not have us sitting together. The bathroom on the bus wA broken. And worse yet the location was a joke. And did I mention the FOOD SUCKED. They ran out of the $30 pineapple drinks. I wish I could select zero stars!

Folks save your money!!! And thank me later!!!

reviews.author: withgaeun
reviews.text: The show was top notch and please go for the show and not for the food. Chief is so funny and the quality of the show covers mediocre food. Though, the price is high, my group enjoyed every second of it.

reviews.author: johncd2020
reviews.text: The show was amazing! From the minute you walk in, you are greeted by the dancers. They give a little background on the show. You the. Take a picture that you can purchase. After the picture, you are escorted to your table. They have tables labeled with your gifts. The food is not bad at all. I recommend going through the everything line so you can try it all. The show was by far the best part. The only bad thing about this luau is the drinks. They have a small and watery mai tai (1 ticket) and

reviews.author: MKThinks
reviews.text: The show was awesome. The food was good but not amazing. The drinks were good too. I feel I got my money's worth. They did have it set up where they served you.

reviews.author: LCBryant227
reviews.text:
So the positives. The chief is hilarious. The dancing is intricate and explains all the different nuances between the cultures from a male and female perspective. The best part is the fire dance at the end! 

The cons. The drink service is slow. If you order the royal package you wont get to use all your tickets. The food is cold when it is served to you. They show you a precooked roasted pig so you dont get that experience. And its far away from Waikiki so you will sit in traffic to get there.

reviews.author: Diane R
reviews.text: We didn't know what to expect at a luau.  The Chief's Luau has great reviews on Trip Advisor so we decided to attend.  We made reservations in advance and miraculously arrived on time.  I can't insist on how important it is to give yourself plenty of time due to traffic.  There was plenty of parking and we were greeted with either a lei of shells or beads.  There was a bar available and we learned how to make coconut leaf crowns that we took home.  There was also small tables of carved wood item

reviews.author: Debbie W
reviews.text: I have a really good time.  They had a craft for us to make.  The food was as good as the entertainers.

reviews.author: bct1090
reviews.text:
El lugar está bastante lejos del hotel, como a 1 hora y la verdad donde hacen el show no me parece el mejor ambiente para un Luau hawaiano, aún así la experiencia es linda, la comida buena, el show del fuego es increíble.

Ojo cuando les ofrezcan elegir los asientos, no vale la pena elegir ROYAL que es lo máximo, en nuestro caso elegimos ALOHA y la vista es buena y gozas de los mismos beneficios practicamente.

reviews.author: Steven S
reviews.text: This was my second luau and I felt it was better than the first luau. It was smaller and because of that it felt more like family. Chief, as well as the entire company, made the experience very enjoyable. When in Honolulu, do as the locals do!

reviews.author: therobertbean263
reviews.text: Spent a lot of time looking online and speaking to the locals about which luau was the best.   By far, most everyone said Chief's.  We looked at others that were a little less expensive, but after looking at all of their websites, photos and reviews we landed here.  If this is your only luau, this is the one to choose.  If you want to see more than one, make this one your last.   It is an incredible experience and my wife and I had literally one of the best nights of our lives.  The richness of

reviews.author: Sawyerfam5
reviews.text: The Chief's Luau was a family highlight for us on our recent trip to Hawaii. Recommended by our hotel, this event did not disappoint. We opted for the cheaper rate per head (as there are 5 of us) and still got a fantastic table and seats very close to the stage. The chief was entertaining as well as informative about the pacific islanders and Polynesian countries. The dancers and especially the fire dancers were colourful, amazing at their craft and full of energy. You could see on the faces of

reviews.author: 237yolandaa
reviews.text: This was my first time going to a luau and it won’t be my last. Depending on how much you are looking to pay is where you are seated and How many drink tickets you receive. My friend and I paid the lowest fee and we had very good seats. There was more than enough food and drink. The food was well prepared and the entertainment was wonderful.

reviews.author: Dimitra D
reviews.text: Overall one of the best Luau's I have been to.  The Chief was the most humbling and welcoming, it was like we were part of the extended family.  Not only was the show spectacular and entertaining, everyone got to be part of the show and of course how can I forget the mai tai's and food, deliscious all round.

reviews.author: CindiLouWho87
reviews.text:
We attended the luau on a Sunday night and had a fantastic time. The show it's self was excellent. Extremely entertaining and the performers were very friendly. Definitely recommend this luau as  a must-see while in Hawaii.  

Many of the people in attendance seemed to thoroughly enjoy the food.  My thoughts on the food were that it was average and pretty typical of a buffet situation. Definitely nothing wrong with the food, just was not that appealing to me.

We purchased the Royal package and

reviews.author: nutnataliatmv
reviews.text: Muy bonito espéctaculo y rica comida, desde el trayecto en el camión te comentan cosas importantes de la cultura hawaiiana. Estábamos en los asientos "ALOHA" y la verdad se ve bien, no considero que valga la pena pagar la diferencia tan eleveda entre una sección y otra. Algo que nos decepcionó un poco es que no habían todas las actividades que mencionaban en la página; sólo era una (corona con palmas) pero decía que serían varias (hula hula, tiro al blanco, lanzas con fuego, etc). Tal vez, si ti

reviews.author: trinacase12
reviews.text:
This luau is a Family friendly Luau . One of including all ages to participate from the youngest to the oldest.
Talking about culture and the value of marriage and family

reviews.author: Miguel Trujillo
reviews.text: Great time. Great vibe. Great people. Thank you

reviews.author: Chris Jones
reviews.text: This was my first Luau. It was a lot of fun. They had a nice buffet and 1 alcoholic drink or soda included. The dancing was amazing. The Chief was funny and engaging. I would recommend this Luau for the experience.

reviews.author: Brian Creek
reviews.text: This is so authentic, educational, entertaining, exciting, and fun. My wife, and I, and our two teenage kids all were completely thrilled with the entire experience.  Could not recommend more highly!

reviews.author: Mrs. ME
reviews.text: 2 stars because staff was friendly and the entertainers tried they really did. However this luau needs to change location the water park was not a good place. It’s very far. The bus will not get there on time and makes a lot of unnecessary stops. The food was just ok you might like only on item. It’s cafeteria food with only one dessert option. And it wasn’t good. Bathrooms are far away and super dirty because again this is a water park. I didn’t even feel like trying the drinks. Now the worst p

reviews.author: Matt Odom
reviews.text: We loved every second of it! We took our 10 and 7 year old kids and they had a blast! The only Luau we’ve ever been to…and the only one we’ll go to!

reviews.author: John Enright
reviews.text: Amazing experience. I had been 10 years ago when they were at Sea Life Park. Food and show were amazing. Great way to start our honeymoon. Was touched to be apart of the show as being celebrated for being a new couple along with all the anniversary couples. Great for families and couples

reviews.author: Keylan Graham
reviews.text: Really amazing show! Performers and staff were great! The food was tasty and it was fun to try some things I'd never had before

reviews.author: L Zan
reviews.text: Fabulous show! The food was great, everybody was nice and polite,  I would recommend it 100%

reviews.author: Will Sullivan
reviews.text: First luau, would come back, food was good but the hosting and show was fire!

reviews.author: Smita Shrestha
reviews.text: Overall, the cultural experience was amazing 10/10. Unfortunately, the food was very very disappointing 0/10. Considering the price we paid, it really wasn’t worth it.

reviews.author: Jennifer
reviews.text: We had a great time! The food was really good and the show was amazing!  My only critique would be there was no signage on where to wait before going in. It seemed a bit disorganized.  But other than that, a great time!

reviews.author: Jim McLochlin
reviews.text: So far this was the highlight of our trip to Hawaii

reviews.author: Chantal Logston
reviews.text: What a fun thing to do. Food was good, drinks good, the show was the best.

reviews.author: LeAndra McDaniels
reviews.text: Chief's Luau was amazing. I learned so much about the culture, the show was entertaining, and the food was amazing. The High Chief was HILARIOUS! The only bummer was the inability to choose your lei. They gave you a lei based on the package you paid for.

reviews.author: B Sh
reviews.text: I would have given it a 5 star but the food was below average I would say. Else the fire show at the end was the best part. Please work on decent food and cocktails please! Else everything was fantastic!

reviews.author: Melvin Dizon
reviews.text:
I recently attended Chief’s Luau in Kapolei, held along Farrington Highway on Oʻahu’s west side, and it offers one of the more lively and entertaining cultural dinner experiences on the island. Luaus are a well-known part of Hawaiʻi’s visitor culture, combining food, storytelling, and performance to introduce guests to Polynesian traditions.

The atmosphere begins building well before the show starts. Guests arrive to a festive environment with cultural demonstrations, music, and interactive act

reviews.author: Marni Stone
reviews.text: Everything about our experience was outstanding! What an incredible family… full of tradition, knowledge and skill that they are eager to share with the world! The food and drinks were delicious and the entertainment was top class! From the humor to the dancing to the fire show… all of it is a 10/10!

reviews.author: Judy Duffy
reviews.text:
We had an outstanding experience at Chief’s Luau in Kapolei. From the moment we arrived, the entire cast was welcoming, gracious, and genuinely hospitable.

Chief himself was hilarious—quick-witted, engaging, and incredibly good at bringing the audience in. He didn’t just entertain; he taught about island culture, traditions, and history in a way that was both informative and fun.

The performers—both the men and women—were exceptional. The dancing, fire knife performance, band, and overall prod

reviews.author: Ken Murrell
reviews.text:
This was our third luau and by far our favorite.  It provides a true learning experience with basket weaving and fire starting.  The dancing was amazing, the food was great, and chief was hilarious.

It is worth every penny.  We did the royal seating.

reviews.author: dlsmerko
reviews.text: The day we were to originally go, they closed due to excessive rain.  We had to book the next weekend and of course it was mostly booked.  We had to go with the Ambassador package.  Though it was very expensive we had a wonderful experience.  One person in our group was in a wheelchair and our guide Enor, was fantastic with making sure he could hear and see everything we did.  We got to do many activities and see many shows and our guide was very knowledgable about all six of the cultures.

reviews.author: Quest403767
reviews.text: We had a wonderful experience at the Polynesian cultural center touring the islands and watching the shows. All were enlightening and enjoyable. The only problem was the terrible drive back with Robert’s bus on the early 6:00 shuttle. Terrible driver with the small shuttle provided on the way back. He blew over the speed bumps and drove way too fast for the conditions. The whole drive back to Waikīkī beach was bumpy. My back and neck were so “out” after  that ride, I had to see my chiropractor w

reviews.author: MequonPaul
reviews.text: Everyone was friendly, gracious  & welcoming. We were interested in conversing with the staff, finding out their names, where they were from etc

reviews.author: lancep988
reviews.text: One of the best buffets I have experienced. Kent was a great server! He was attentive and kind. So helpful.

reviews.author: melodieg242
reviews.text: Kent was our buffet server, great service !!!

reviews.author: hkdi
reviews.text: The experience was fantastic! The good was delicious and Kent was our server who did such a great job! He is such a kind person and helpful.

reviews.author: cltb11
reviews.text: Enjoyed visiting with family. Donnie in the buffet area was friendly and provided great service

reviews.author: 108tede
reviews.text: Enjoyed the guided canoe ride.

reviews.author: nancybX1149HX
reviews.text: Great time learning about all the islands. Staff was so welcoming and helpful

reviews.author: lancem526
reviews.text: So great, Benji our waiter was very attentive and kind, especially to my picky kids. Food has improved since last time I came, and was very high quality.

reviews.author: Roam535401
reviews.text: Absolutely incredible experience that lasted from 12:00 in the afternoon all the way till 9:00 at night with constant entertainment and talent. The food was delicious and the guides were wonderful. We had Kia and Hailey as our guides and James as our waiter for the luau. Awesome!! Totally worth the money, and especially knowing it is helping these young adults pay for their schooling!

reviews.author: C6130DYcarolm
reviews.text: Great.  Eric was a fantastic tour guide. He was very knowledgeable and accommodating. Good job.  We also received excellent service from Jared at the buffet.  Very attentive thanks for a great time.

reviews.author: LSHop84
reviews.text:
We had an amazing tour guide Ryan! We loved him and appreciate all the knowledge he had! Great guy! Our buffet dinner was also amazing! Our server/hostess Aly did an amazing job!
Great experience overall learning about so many amazing cultures!

reviews.author: 247noab
reviews.text: Jared was amazing, show was amazing , food was amazing thank you so much!

reviews.author: dancL1322BT
reviews.text: The guided tour was very informative and a lot of fun. The Lu’au and shows were great.

reviews.author: 815tr
reviews.text: We had a wonderful time with Kaleb our tour guide. He made the experience. I wouldn’t have done this without the guide. Strong strong recommend. Dinner and luau was fun, average food but clean and totally acceptable.

reviews.author: R2330ZFcrystals
reviews.text: A fun experience for the family! Emursed in rich culture all day. The luau was delicious. Jared was a wonderful server and made my daughter's birthday extra special.

reviews.author: angelicamariah2016
reviews.text: Excellent location, infrastructure and service. Great for kids, families and adults. This place worths every dollar that you pay. It is not cheap but it is excellent. You receive what you pay.

reviews.author: 582skipj
reviews.text: Great service and friendly people.  Very educational and fun.

reviews.author: jacksonbD7769HZ
reviews.text: I was delighted to learn that all of the money we spent for the ticket went to the tuition of students at BYU Hawaii! This was such a great experience! Highly recommend!!

reviews.author: Geena Hanson
reviews.text: Booking a VIP tour is a must! Ask for Ivan! The best knowledge and fun filled day experiencing true cultures. The luau and breath of life are a must see.

reviews.author: Prabin Rokaya
reviews.text: The centre provides amazing exposure and insights to several Pacific islands culture and way of life! Many villages have hands on training and games which gives an immersive experience. One would learn a lot about Polynesian culture in one visit than several text books. It is ideal to allocate one whole day for the visit.

reviews.author: Mike Lee
reviews.text:
I had an absolutely incredible time at the Polynesian Cultural Center! It is easily one of the most immersive and educational experiences you can find. The villages are beautiful, and the staff's passion for sharing their heritage is truly inspiring.

I wanted to give a special shout-out to our tour guide, Koon, from Forever Hawaii Cafe. It was incredibly helpful and well-organized, allowing us to navigate the center with ease while still getting all the deep insights and history we were looking

reviews.author: Heidi Hoyle
reviews.text: Ivan was our amazing guide with the Super Ambassador package.  Awesome!  Best part was Ha show!!!!

reviews.author: Amador823
reviews.text: Very cheesy presentations.  Dad jokes were prevelant in EVERY talk, every demonstration.  Like Jungle Cruise level jokes. Several of the presentations really offered nothing educational.  The Tongan drum show was literally three volunteers being pulled up on stage, teased a little for their inability to follow along with the drummer. And that's it. 10min and done. More laughs than culture.  It's a very clean, polished and inauthentic amusement park feel.  Would not recommend.

reviews.author: Roger S Alama Sr
reviews.text: The Polynesian culture center is a must place to see enjoy all the Island cultures Hawaii, Samoa, Tahiti, Fiji, beautiful people, friendly, courteous, always helpful

reviews.author: Jessica Fields
reviews.text: Came here with our 5 and 6.5 year old and it was great. Definitely enough happening to keep us all entertained for several hours.

reviews.author: Heather Hairbows
reviews.text:
This was one of the highlights for our trip to Hawaii!! I still talk about this cultural center and recommend it to anyone going to Hawaii.

We can’t wait to go back and visit again for our next trip.

reviews.author: Lisa Estrada
reviews.text: Amelia was wonderful!!!

reviews.author: Chris Hurst
reviews.text: It was raining like crazy when we visited. I hardly noticed. They provided us with a poncho. We enjoyed the various displays and shows on each island. It was  great way to learn about the culture, the islands and amazing people of Polynesian descent. The luau was fantastic. The show at night was great as well. The staff were very friendly and talkative.

reviews.author: J. Tucker Mills
reviews.text:
One of the best experiences on the island.
A great way for the whole family to learn the culture of the islands, and have a great time doing it.

reviews.author: Colton Black
reviews.text: Wow! What a place! They put in an amazing show. One of my highest recommendations of things to do when on Oahu. You get to learn about cultures, see amazing performances, music. Make sure to staff for the Buffet/Luau and show at the end. A truly unique experience

reviews.author: Shayle Sowa
reviews.text: Very good, shows were amazing especially the Fiji one

reviews.author: Mi Pho
reviews.text: I am super impressed of the place because all the villages, hands-on activities and performances !👍

reviews.author: Lesia Skillman
reviews.text: Jann was our tour guide and she was amazing. She had a lot of information on each culture and was willing to answer our questions. The whole place is fun, entertaining, and rich with information on the culture. We will be back!!

reviews.author: Lenny Coss
reviews.text: Food was poor quality. Most of the time was spent trying to sell you something. Drinks were watered down. Isn't worth the money by far

reviews.author: Nick Jordan
reviews.text: The description said on the beach and showed actually on the beach. However it was indoors. Very disappointing based off the pictures. Plus the resort was under construction.

reviews.author: Ivan Rabulan
reviews.text:
Nice Luau experience. Food is just okay, nothing special. Typical of what you get on a mass dining experience. Cocktail drinks were pretty basic too.

Show was good, nothing really out of this world. Good to do when in Hawaii but we probably wont do it again.

reviews.author: Diego Carvalho
reviews.text: Best for families and couples alike. I was touched by their professionalism at accommodating my son with sensory issues. Cona did an excellent job! Our experience was memorable! We loved the show. Food was fresher and tastier than other luau's that we have attended. Thanks!

reviews.author: marj1st
reviews.text: They do a nice job of keeping everyone engaged - free flowers for your hair or temporary tattoos. The show was fine with about 8 dancers/participants, all of whom were good sports though not overly friendly. The emcee had plenty of dad jokes but he was entertaining. The included drinks were watered down heavily, as expected. The food was decent, not exceptional, as expected. They took your photo in front of the beach but the pics were remarkably as awful as they were overpriced, also as expected

reviews.author: Larissa Ham
reviews.text: My first time going to a luau and it was a great experience. From the music to the dancing and food  I suggest getting VIP seating . Mahalo

reviews.author: Gregory Burkett
reviews.text: I attended tonight. Every aspect was outstanding. You didn’t have to leave your seat for alcoholic drinks or nonalcoholic drinks. Servers came by frequently. offering drinks. If they didn’t have exactly what you wanted they would go to the bar and promptly bring it to you. The buffet was delicious and the line moved quickly. You didn’t have to go back for more because the plates were gigantic. Seriously you have never seen a plate so big. The live band started quickly and the main entertainment

reviews.author: Aaron Jenner
reviews.text: With luaus it can be different, some have good food but lack in the entertainment, others are the opposite. This luau had great food, great show, and great service. It was a great experience and show of culture. Would pay to see again. Thank you for the great night.

reviews.author: Janet Lahr
reviews.text: Enjoyed the meal and entire show learning the history and culture of the Pacific Islanders! Definitely worth it!

reviews.author: Christopher Lyons
reviews.text: I was very skeptical on going to a Luau but am glad I did.  The music, traditions, and history taught were all fun and interesting to learn.  The food and drinks are great too!

reviews.author: Joey Berkland
reviews.text: The show started 30-40 minutes later than the scheduled time and was a slow moving show. Food was fantastic but also very short lived and minimally an “all you care to eat buffet”. Great all you could drink drinks for a hour or so. Would not recommend to the first time luau goer.

reviews.author: Brandon Foote
reviews.text: Family oriented.  Food was great.  Entertainment was captivating and table service was terrific.  Lovely sunset overlooking the ocean.  Pretty dreamy.

reviews.author: Lynn Tiilikka
reviews.text: Fabulous family experience.  The music was good.  The food was delious!  The staff was accommodating and the location along the ocean with a sunset!  Would go again!

reviews.author: Ramon Morales
reviews.text: Pretty great to check out. The performance was more impressive than the food for me. The food is very Traditional hawaiin food which was cool to try and it is served in a buffet style. I also only have a photo of the sunset as I was too focused on the performance and story telling.

reviews.author: Nick D
reviews.text: Great storyline, venue was excellent and made a dry environment despite the significant rain.

reviews.author: 899marleenb
reviews.text: We really enjoyed the show, they did a fantastic job!!

reviews.author: ShawnF1
reviews.text: Way over crowded, they herd you like animals. Food was awful!

reviews.author: Mahanatma K
reviews.text: Aloha🌸The show was amazing , food was great , and  train ride was fun . Staff is awesome  ,mohalo

reviews.author: ponkyjoe
reviews.text:
We've gone to this Luau twice and would go again.
The food is delicious and plentiful.  Poi is available, if desired (it is!!).  The craft fair is small but of good quality.  Open bar.  Open air but covered - a big deal in the rainy season!
The show is an excellent and artistic 'story' of the coming from Tahiti to Hawaii - beautiful and extremely well done, though not the typical Polynesian Revue of most Luaus...... there is some of that, however, before dinner.

We also included the train ride

reviews.author: patriciajC773GR
reviews.text: food OK for a huge buffet, show was incredibly good.

reviews.author: Kelly B
reviews.text: The luau is a great experience. The buffet dinner is good and they get people served quickly. The show is unique and beautiful to watch. The performers interact with and engage the audience. There are local vendors outside before the show begins.

reviews.author: Jessica O
reviews.text: Show was fantastic. The whole service staff and dancers were top notch and very friendly. Dinner was good, and the Mai tais were amazing! One thing to note was that you can’t go wrong with any seating. We were able to see everything from our table. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Adrienne064
reviews.text: Really enjoyed the story telling and the theatrics behind it. Beautifully done! Food was ok. But I don’t go to Luaus for the food as much as the “theatre”.  We really enjoyed the local vendors area where high quality local items were sold. Drinks were great!

reviews.author: stephanie c
reviews.text: Luau Kalamaku at the Historic Kilohana Plantation in Lihue is a beautiful, authentic Hawaiian experience set on a stunning 104-acre property. The hula performances and storytelling were meaningful and engaging, the staff was warm and welcoming, and the buffet offered a great variety of traditional dishes. Everything is included in one price, making it easy, and well worth it.

reviews.author: DeniseR1404
reviews.text: The show and organization that was there was wonderful!  Our driver, Ms. Kay was on time and fun to have to and from the Luau!

reviews.author: IzziePlayer
reviews.text: While we enjoyed the show, I was not impressed with the buffet selections for the price that we paid per person for this event. If you like to drink alcohol, drinks were included, so that was a plus, though drink selections were also limited.  I was very happy that it was covered though, as we did get rain that day. They did have professional photographers ready to take your pictures, which was nice. Though, again, very pricey.

reviews.author: Jackie W
reviews.text: Loved the story, music and dancing. Food was delicious. The service was good too. Enjoyed the train ride and crafters. Recommend

reviews.author: Donna G
reviews.text:
Their attentiiveness and kindness went above and beyond. The pork and teriyaki chicken were delicious. 
The show was a beautiful story. It held the crowds attention.  Highly recommend

reviews.author: Dee P
reviews.text: The show was very entertaining. The performers are extremely talented. The show had a story line which I enjoyed. I would definitely recommend going.

reviews.author: Suzanne C
reviews.text:
This amazing event acted out the history of the people of Hawaii, as well as their dances and music, all of which were stunning. 

There were local artists selling their products before the Lu’au started, and the photographs and jewellery were all stunning. I could have spent a fortune, but had to limit myself, unfortunately!

We had a very good buffet dinner, which was delicious, with an open bar included, while the music and dancing entertained us, but once everyone had finished eating, the ma

reviews.author: akb5150
reviews.text: The show and amenities were great! This show included more artistic, broadway influence performances. The food was typical luau food.  The open bar was severely lacking with  minimal drink options.  Only 2 wine and 2 beer options. The liquor drinks were too sweet and cheap tasting. Even if they added a cash bar with  premium options to include better beer and wine options, I wouldn’t have minded paying more.

reviews.author: Leslie N
reviews.text: Overall, the entertainment was pretty good, but the rest of it seemed a bit unorganized, and the food was nothing special.

reviews.author: DoctorChuck
reviews.text: The show was very good, have seen multiple before.  If you've done other luaus highly suggest the dinner package including the Gaylord restaurant pre- show.  Great food

reviews.author: mlrericka
reviews.text: The train ride was awesome and the show was fantastic!  The food was also really great!  10/10 I would go again!

reviews.author: todd A
reviews.text: Highly recommend. It was incredible, food was great, the show was awesome, and staff made everyone feel welcome and cared for. Royal Kona does a Luau buy no where as entertaining or as good of food

reviews.author: jamontre archie
reviews.text: The story telling and dance along with the set were phenomenal! As my first luau this was an amazing introduction to cultural appreciation.

reviews.author: Leigh Hensley
reviews.text: Great musicians, singing, history and performances! The show was so entertaining while also educating us all on Hawaiian history and heritage. The performers looked so joyful while performing... I just loved seeing the smiles on their faces. The food and service were wonderful, and I appreciated that it was well-staffed and very organized! Thank you.

reviews.author: Amy-G Mehrle
reviews.text: Wonderful luau for the entire family. Food was delicious and plentiful, even choices for little ones! The entertainment was traditional Hawaiian style and nicely done! Very enjoyable evening for the entire family. We paid extra for the front row, which was nice too!

reviews.author: James Oldenhoff
reviews.text: Food was amazing but the luau its self was not the best I’ve seen. Very underwhelming and I feel time it didn’t have a story line as most of the ones I’ve been to have had.

reviews.author: Ariel Robinson
reviews.text: Beautiful setting and a big contrast to the huge corporate luaus at other resorts we’ve been to.  Very relaxing and pleasant, no long lines, quality show.

reviews.author: Mojo Jo jo
reviews.text: I don’t think it could have been any better.  Outstanding show!  Music singing and dance with a description of Hawaiian history and culture.

reviews.author: sahana prabhu
reviews.text:
Not worth it at all!! Please don’t waste your money on this.
First of all it’s shared tables with random people
Which I didn’t expect
Especially if you are vegetarians as they will only give plain rice and raw veggies and cut papayas in the name of appetizer .
Totally disappointed

reviews.author: Josh Mitchell
reviews.text: We signed up for this Luau that was supposed to be from 5:30-8:00 pm. We were in line at 5:20 and did not get into the venue until 5:50. The line was super backed up and moving slow. When we entered the venue they were already beginning the show and sent us straight to get our food. People were moving around and getting food while the show was going on which was very distracting. Around 7:10 the show wrapped up. People in our group hadn't even had a chance to get dessert or a second drink. Reall

reviews.author: Amy Henderson
reviews.text: Wonderful show and great food!  We thoroughly enjoyed this luau and highly recommend it.  The dancers are very talented and told a wonderful story.  We really enjoyed the buffet as well, lots of delicious food and drinks!

reviews.author: Livelyupyourbreath .c
reviews.text: I really enjoyed the evening. The setting was beautiful and, overall, the food was tasty. Excellent MC.

reviews.author: Cynthia Sheehan
reviews.text: Wonderful spirit, food and dancing. This is the best on the island. Lots of fun and wonderful party atmosphere mixed with impressive music and dancing. Food was wonderful and plentiful. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Jason Kester
reviews.text: If I could give them 10/5 I would. We went to the luau for my birthday and it couldn't have been more magical. The food was to die for especially the poke station and Kalua pork! The story telling and dancing were more than magical. I will be forever grateful to all the performers, cooks, waiter/waitresses, bartenders and everyone involved for creating such a wonderful night!

reviews.author: kelly craig
reviews.text: Never been to a lua. It met all my expectations.  All performers were fun.food was ok. I was there for the entertainment.  Loved every show.

reviews.author: Jerry Deng
reviews.text: There were too many flies in the area attacking the food. Also seating area is cramped even for VIP package.

reviews.author: S J
reviews.text: This luau was very organized, accommodating and well rounded.  There were activities for adults and children, the food was amazingly good for a group setting and the show was entertaining.  The open bar was a plus; no long lines and a good selection.  The only negative was parking.  Our ticket was validated but the scanner didn’t recognize it so it cost us $15.70 to get out of the parking lot.  No one answered the ‘help’ button and that is why I rated service as 4 rather than 5 stars.  Otherwise

reviews.author: H8704LFangiet
reviews.text: I was on the fence about attending a Luau while in Kauai but I'M SO HAPPY WE DID!  The Smith Family Luau was such an unforgettable experience.  The grounds/gardens were beautiful and the Imu ceremony was so memorable.  My kids loved exploring the gardens and feeding the birds!  The food and drinks definitely exceeded our expectations- so delicious!  The show was so entertaining and culturally engaging!  I could feel the Ohana!  The price of admission was definitely worth the experience!

reviews.author: G5205CPsteves
reviews.text: The luau was a great way for us to spend our last evening on Kaua’i. We arrived as they opened the entrance to the beautiful gardens. We were given a shell lei and offered a tram ride through the gardens. We opted to walk and enjoyed the different garden spaces, water features and lots of peacocks and geese wandering the grounds. Dinner was in a large covered space. Most of the tables were reserved for families/groups. It was not easy to find out where to sit as a twosome, but it did work out. T

reviews.author: f0hf
reviews.text: Do it, you won't be disappointed. Worth the money, worth the wait, worth every penny. Book early!

reviews.author: PatrickJ1292
reviews.text: Incredible Evening from start to finish.  Beautiful 30 acre grounds unlike going to a luau at a hotel.  My wife has celiac disease and must have gluten free food.  I contacted them ahead of time and they went ABOVE AND BEYOND to accommodate her dietary needs.  Kamika Smith (general manager and owner) was a pleasure to talk with as well.  Not only are all the items clearly marked "gluten free" on the buffet lines - they also made a special plate of gluten free items delivered to our table.  Some

reviews.author: gbcosner
reviews.text: The website says go early and see the botanical gardens. They are closed prior to checkin. There is nothing else in the area to see or do.

reviews.author: Kimberly S
reviews.text: Amazing!  Loved everything about this experience - from the stories to the food to the luau to the botanical gardens - it was memorable!

reviews.author: Mommy1Indiana
reviews.text: Along with renting kayaks to Secret Falls, this was the favorite part of our trip for our family (2 teen boys). The gardens are amazingly beautiful and they take you on a trolley ride to share info about the plants and flowers. Dinner and drinks were great, and they don’t go light on their pours for the alcohol options. You can buy a bag of food to feed the birds. The imu presentation is cool and the actual dance was amazing. Just loved everything about this so much! I had contacted them last mi

reviews.author: jennycE6891MX
reviews.text:
If you’re looking for an authentic Hawaiian luau in Kauai, the Smith Family Luau is as authentic as it gets. Don’t let the name fool you. This is a multi generational Hawaiian family luau. You’re welcomed into beautiful, lush gardens, and arriving early is a must to enjoy the scenery, trolley ride, native birds, and animals. My kids especially loved feeding the ducks.

The food was excellent. Everything was ono (my only complaint was eating too much!). The evening ends with an incredible show th

reviews.author: jeffreya217
reviews.text: Just an absolutely first class operation all the way around.  Amazing, friendly service.  Really good food.  Gardens and atmosphere are awesome.  Show is excellent.  Really just had an amazing time.  Disney-level organization and excellence (meant as a compliment).

reviews.author: RobinAlaska
reviews.text: My husband and I did this luau on Christmas Eve and had a great time! The people were very friendly, the food was amazing (especially the pork), and the show was very good! About the only thing I could complain about are the seats at the show.  While they all have a great view of the stage and are padded, they are not very comfortable. After sitting for a hour, my behind was a bit sore!

reviews.author: dangerdave
reviews.text: This place has been doing this for over 80 years and it shows. They moved 400 people through a garden tour, full dinner and dinner entertainment, and on to an amphitheater of a full blown show in 3 hours.  Like a well oiled machine. Food was actually pretty good too. If you want to do a Luau, this is the only real choice on Kauai.

reviews.author: Julia C
reviews.text:
When working hard trip to Kawai. We decided to do more than one luau. The Smith family luau seem to be a popular one so we went ahead and booked that along with the Ahi Lele Fire Show ( which we actually preferred.)

Last night we attended the Smith Family Luau.  Since we had both attended luaus on other islands we knew what to expect. Smith family is your basic luau with music and good food. The property is actually a botanical garden and it is stunning. When you enter, they take a picture with

reviews.author: spg2023x
reviews.text: This event was too large for my tastes.  With over 300 people attending, it was too much like an assembly line.  The food was okay but not special.  The show after dinner was entertaining.

reviews.author: erikad277
reviews.text: The grounds were absolutely beautiful and we enjoyed the tram ride around the garden getting to learn about it. I loved that they take a picture when you enter, however where I’m paying $30 for a picture I would have preferred to have the option of it just being our family and not the hula dancers. There was also honestly a TON of people which again understandable but also makes it a little uncomfortable being so crammed. The food was good, drinks were good and the show was good. I did feel that

reviews.author: catherinecT4782TR
reviews.text: First, beautiful grounds…way too many chickens, constant pest level. My dad lives in Hawaii and this is the 3 luau I have been to, first on this island. Nothing authentic at this one in many ways. It felt like a tourist mill place. Food was like a cafeteria; served in stainless steel serving trays, bland, and while items were traditional there was a minimum selection serving “safe tourist” food. Tables like a kids summer camp to get fed and moved on. Lacked decor, warmth and atmosphere. Loud and

reviews.author: Tree1234567891
reviews.text: I celebrated my birthday at the Smith Family Luau with my husband and 16 year old son.  Beforehand, we took the boat ride over to the Fern Grotto, which was relaxing, and informative.  As this was our first luau, we didn't have a point of comparison, but we found the Smith family garden to be a perfect backdrop for the experience.  Upon arrival, we took a tram ride through the garden and then were dropped off and given time to explore the gardens at our own pace, including all the lively birds.

reviews.author: rain1939
reviews.text: The staff is kind and amazing and made our family trip one to remember for a lifetime. We did the fern grotto boat trip first-very worth it- went on a little property tram that thrilled our 3yr old and adults with beautiful peacocks that performed miraculously and walked about gardens and cool orchid greenhouse. The meal was soooo tasty and unfortunately for my waistline, a buffet where they encourage you to eat more! Fun drinks with a surprising open bar, and live music.  The show seating area

reviews.author: 5rsfa
reviews.text: “Luau” was at the top of our list of things to do while visiting Kauai.  Smiths was recommended in a guidebook, so we made our reservation.  SO GLAD WE DID!  From the moment we drove onto the property, we enjoyed an excellent production.  Definitely felt like we were being welcomed by family.  They must host hundreds of guests, but it never feels crowded or rushed.  We started with the tram ride and then moved on to the pig roast ceremony.  From there, the crowd descends upon the dining area - b

reviews.author: F3432DHkellyp
reviews.text: Gardens were pretty. Luau was great and lots of history. Food was some of the best we had all week.  If you have 5 or more, you get reserved seating which was nice. $30 for the picture was a little crazy.

reviews.author: irismaea2023
reviews.text:
We had a wonderful time at the Smith Family Luau! The parking was very well-organized, with clear directions on where to go, which made the whole arrival process easy. Checking in was a breeze, and the photo op was quick and fun. The tram ride around the property was nice, though we wished it lasted a bit longer to take in more of the beautiful surroundings.

The area where the ceremony was held felt a bit tight, and we would have loved more space to get a better view of the roasted pig ceremony

reviews.author: Forest Barkdoll-Weil
reviews.text:
I’m not the right audience for this Luau experience. The Luau felt commercialized. The performers were great, but the ambience with show lights and big tables surrounding the stage made me think of a commercialized cruise experience.

If you are the resort type of tourist, this may be the perfect luau for you. If you are into more intimate and authentic experiences, this probably isn’t for you.

reviews.author: David Willar
reviews.text: Great show, was interesting to have the dancing and music to describe the voyage to HI. The food was good but it would have been nice to have pineapple. The bar tenders did a great job, fast service and did not skimp on the rum! Roy did an exceptional job and made my grandkids feel welcome.

reviews.author: Jesse Parenteau
reviews.text: The pork was a little salty for me because I hardly ever use salt but the rest was tremendous

reviews.author: Lo Vo
reviews.text: We were so far back, we couldn't see the stage. By the time the salad got to us, all that was left was rotten tomatoes and a few pieces of white lettuce. Three toilets for hundreds of women. Worst luau I've ever experienced and one of the most expensive.  Skip it!

reviews.author: Becky Wittmer
reviews.text: Absolutely loved everything about it!  Everything was so organized, the food was delicious & normal & the hula just awesome! I had a wonderful time!!!

reviews.author: Neal Sieglaff
reviews.text: Four in our group, all had a good time, appreciate the story in dance and so g, food was great, staff very friendly and helpful.

reviews.author: Holly Leivas
reviews.text: My family loved this luau and beautiful performance! We are so glad we came!

reviews.author: Diane Salcedo
reviews.text: Shelter from rain which is a common occurrence friendly atmosphere show tells a story. Always enjoy when we come

reviews.author: Teresa Mogielnicki
reviews.text: My favorite luau!  Wonderful production, well organized efficient and delicious buffet, attentive and friendly staff.  I'm a happy, repeat visitor.

reviews.author: Jamie Potter
reviews.text: The pork you are served is not the pig you see being unearthed, it was pretty salty. The chicken was fantastic and so was the potato mac salad. Desserts were a huge hit pineapple upside down cake, haupia and banana tarts. The show was amazing!! Beautiful story. There is an open bar but it closes at 7:30 when the show starts, which didn't bother us! Overall a wonderful experience, I finally got a lei so that really made my night!

reviews.author: Jacklyn Ryan
reviews.text: Great experience to book. Food was surprisingly good, it was just limited for how long you could get food and they cut off drinks early too. Good performance though. Never received the photos, so not super happy I have to email but we’ll see. Would recommend overall.

reviews.author: Sheduff
reviews.text: We had a great time at Lu’au Kalamaku. They were very efficient at getting so many people in, seated, fed, entertained, and on their way. Everyone was friendly and helpful and the show was amazing. The leis that were given to us upon entry were beautiful and I was unaware that it was an open bar when I booked so that was a nice surprise. We do not eat pork and that was one of the main dishes but they did have chicken as an alternative.  The food wasn't out of this world, but was plentiful and go

reviews.author: Dave Gibbings
reviews.text: Great experience! I highly recommend this lu’au

reviews.author: Jordan
reviews.text: Wonderful show and the food was top notch!

reviews.author: Tim Brown
reviews.text: The grounds were beautiful, and the production was awesome.  The performers did an unbelievable job, and told a beautiful story.  I was especially excited after the Imu unveiling to eat the pork.  However, the pork was unbelievably salty...to the point where our entire table (other parties other than our own) couldn't finish it.  Every bite of meat crunched hard with tons of salt crystals.  Very disappointing and ruined the entire meal.

reviews.author: Randall Johnson
reviews.text: Fabulous evening, fantastic food. Everything made for a perfect end to our family vacation.

reviews.author: Sejal Patel
reviews.text: My whole family enjoyed the Lu'au Kalamaku! The whole experience was amazing! From the train ride to the entertainment, everything was worth it!

reviews.author: Cathy Grimsley
reviews.text: Great service great time at the luau the train ride was great as well. Learning the history of the plantation was a plus. Great seeing all the fruit trees. I’d never seen an avocado tree. Great memories!

reviews.author: Jill Arendt
reviews.text: I guess it was great all around....but please meet Dee.what a fabulous person...went out of her way to make our experience great! And even when I started to get a headache she helped and arranged...personally...our way home....who does this? A friend forever...

reviews.author: Sierra Roath
reviews.text: When you first arrive you take a tram around the property and they show you the layout. When you first enter I guess they sell bird food in baggies for kids to feed the birds I obviously missed it! They then drop you off by the imu where they dig up the pig from the ground. You then can wander the beautiful grounds for a bit then go watch the imu ceremony which was nicely done!!! Then you head across a small bridge to where you eat! It is buffet style and release by tables. They have an open bar

reviews.author: Lara Nosworthy
reviews.text:
I would rate this higher, but on entering the dining space the host of the luau was disrespectful to my family. He made no effort to make amends for his mistake, so instead of a 3 for this luau I would rate it a 2.

Objectively, this luau was not as high quality as some of the others we have been to. Luau kalamaku in lihue, is in my opinion, much better overall. Go to that one if you are in Kauai and looking for a luau.

The food here was ok although the kalua pig was over salted and the dessert

reviews.author: Melissa Duncan
reviews.text: Great time! Lots of fun:)

reviews.author: Taylor
reviews.text: We enjoyed our luau here. The food was good, but we didn’t feel like the drinks had much liquor. It is quite expensive too, with over $100/person. The gardens are nicely maintained and the staff are friendly.

reviews.author: George B
reviews.text: Absolutely phenomenal experience! From the beauty of the gardens to the exceptional food and an amazing show, we loved every bit of it. And a special thanks to the Smith family for their warm hospitality.

reviews.author: Jennifer Stark
reviews.text: We were told this is the best luau and it really was amazing! The botanical garden is gorgeous. Ponds with fish, flowering trees, peacocks, ducks, doves and of course chickens. It starts with a tram ride around the garden. The driver explains what all the trees are and gives a great history of the land. Then you explore the gardens on foot. There is a ceremony to unearth the pig from the ground oven then cocktails and entertainment. Then the feast. Wow! The food was amazing. Fish, pork, steak, h

reviews.author: Jonathan Little
reviews.text: The Smith’s know how to throw a party!! From the digging out of the pig, to the drinks and the show and his experience really showcases the meaning of Ohana. Worth every penny.

reviews.author: John Fowler
reviews.text: We had a great time except for the parking lot attendant!  Our family had a great time at the Smith Family Garden Luau.  The food and entertainment was great.  The setting is beautiful.  One thing that shocked us was the attitude of the parking attendant.  She was rude.  She approached our car as we were parking. told us to stop and then reached in and grabbed the steering wheel because she did not like the position of the front wheels.  Not a great first impression!  Overall we had a great time

reviews.author: Olivia Crueger
reviews.text: We went to the Smith Family Luau on our Honeymoon, but specifically our 5 year anniversary date as well! Every step of the way, staff was helpful, considerate and overall amazing!! The entire experience was curated so well and knowing it's family run and operated was so cool! We loved being immersed in your culture and your family values from start to finish. All the food, drinks, the performances and live music were top notch. Everything was clean, well groomed and so beautiful inside the prope

reviews.author: Shea Eckert
reviews.text: this place was GREAT! extremely entertaining and respected Hawaiian culture. beautiful grounds - the peacocks were magical! The food and drinks were great and a good way to sample Hawaiian cuisine.

reviews.author: Alisa Betts
reviews.text: Does not disappoint!  Tour and history of the lush gardens, revealing the pig, drinks (try them all!,) dinner and the show. Second time we have been here 16 years apart and both times there wasn't a thing I would change.  Everything is efficient and done well. Thank you Smith family!

reviews.author: Joe Hutchings
reviews.text:
Great family owned and run entertainment.
Food was incredible with a great atmosphere.

reviews.author: John Gettys
reviews.text: Best luau show ever. Loved it. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: maria s
reviews.text: Food wasn't that great. The show was kind of boring. Was neat to see there culture though

reviews.author: Tiffany Rupp
reviews.text: If you want a great experience, you have to have the mindset for it. Anything you do with a negative mindset will be negative. The luau is truly an amazing experience. You'll get to explore gorgeous, well maintained gardens, have a lovely Hawaiian buffet, open bar or great virgin drinks, and watch an amazing cultural show to round out the night. Of the three times I've been to this luau this night was my favorite. They have signs by the food to let you know what they contain for major allergies.

reviews.author: Charlene Maciel
reviews.text: Had a most amazing  time manta cultures were represented.

reviews.author: Katie Carlson
reviews.text: By far the BEST Luau I've been to. From the food, entertainment, and the garden, it doesn't get any better than that!!

reviews.author: Joseph L
reviews.text: Justin and his crew were incredible. The yacht was amazing and they paid attention to every detail. High quality snorkeling gear, delicious food (we had the burgers and fish) and high end cocktails. Will definitely do this again!

reviews.author: Z5057AWliliab
reviews.text: Great crew who clearly loves what they do. We only got to experience coral gardens due to weather but we got to whale watch instead of visiting molokini (we saw a mom and her baby)

reviews.author: Liz S
reviews.text: Fun snorkel and whale watching experience on a very large boat in a very big group. Generous open bar and delicious lunch.

reviews.author: mitchellk335
reviews.text: Wonderful time! Bathrooms were clean and the boat wasn’t over packed.

reviews.author: susannL4482EM
reviews.text: I had done a lot of research on which snorkel trip to take to Molokini and am so glad I decided on this one. We went in March when Maui was having a lot of rains and flooding. It cleared up enough to go snorkeling which was decent. We saw an eel, baby shark, sea urchins, and of course plenty of coral and fish! Due to the storms, the water was not good to swim in turtle town so Captain Justin skipped that and instead decided to take us on a whale watching tour which was amazing as we saw so many

reviews.author: 398cheriw
reviews.text: Fantastic experience! Amazing crew and boat - Elua is new and top notch: hot showers, nice bathrooms, great food - but the best part was the crew. They were so helpful and friendly. Even though the weather didn’t cooperate for a full morning of snorkeling, the crew cheerfully turned the trip into an unforgettable whale watching experience. Thank you Captain Justin and crew, for the amazing memories. I’ve been on several different boats out of Maalaea Harbor, and this trip was over the top!!

reviews.author: Jet682192
reviews.text: Great location, good food.  The best part is the crew.  Captain Patrick was very knowlegable.  Life guarda Jim was great explaining all the tupes of fish we saw.  Julie and Maddie also Great.   Made for a memorable fun family day.

reviews.author: rjshim
reviews.text: We travel a lot, and this is absolutely a must-do excursion when in Maui!  We have done other snorkeling & scuba trips in the past, but this crew and boat are incredible.  Worth every penny!!!  The crew from Captain Justin, to Chris, Sienna, Kaden, and Natalia are all top-notch!  The light breakfast and lunch were great, and the cocktails were some of the best we had while in Maui!  I can not say enough good things about our day (and the weather wasn't that great).  If I could give 10 stars, I w

reviews.author: FlackM
reviews.text:
My family had a fantastic time snorkeling at Molokini crater and turtle town. Had some great whale watching on our way which was a bonus. The crew was amazing, super attentive and gracious. The food was great and the boat was very nicely appointed. 

If you don’t want to be crowded or feel overwhelmed, pick this luxury tour. Well worth the extra money. 

I’d go again if I had more time! Both my kids (11 and 15) said it was the best.

reviews.author: rollas2018
reviews.text: Crew was professional and knowledgeable, food was great!, well organized, would highly recommend this dinner cruise 😎😎

reviews.author: 10cja
reviews.text: This was a nice way to spend a day. I wish there were less people on the boat because that would make for a  more rewarding experience for snorkeling. The crew was fantastic and I give them five stars.

reviews.author: D934PIcharlesr
reviews.text: Staff was super nice and helpful. Food and drinks were very good as well. What a beautiful day!

reviews.author: H5423DKalexandera
reviews.text: We had a such an amazing time on the Elua - so many fish, lots of turtles, good equipment, and a fun and helpful crew. The food and drinks were also flowing strongly, making it a perfect day.

reviews.author: carriemX1898MD
reviews.text: My husband and I booked this experience when we arrived in Maui on Thursday, based upon the reviews, and they did not let us down. The crew was exceptional, literally went over the top to ensure everyone was having a great time. This was my first time snorkeling and not being a swimmer, I had some anxiety about doing it. Tiff was amazing!  She talked me through everything and was so kind. I ended up having one of the best days ever!  We saw whales, beautiful fish, coral and turtles!  Food was gr

reviews.author: Camper523730
reviews.text: We chose this experience because of the reviews. It looked like it had the best food and crew and bathrooms and just overall experience. However, it was pricey, so I was worried it wouldn't live up to the reviews. It exceeded them! We had the most fabulous day. We saw whales right after we set out and stopped the boat to watch them spout--a mama, baby, and escort male. Then we snorkeled molokini bay and it was quite the experience. My husband and I are older so we rented the wetsuit jackets and

reviews.author: X7595IHmarkm
reviews.text: Fantastic day, even though it was a little windy. Humpbacks, turtles, clear water and a great crew.

reviews.author: GrandTour53462155152
reviews.text: So happy we booked the Pride of Maui. Smaller group made for an uncrowded boat. And we weren’t crowed in the water either. Have lots of pictures with no one else around. The crew were amazing. When I saw how full the big boats were, this seemed like a no brainer. Thank you to the crew. You were great.

reviews.author: bradaB6583LJ
reviews.text: Great excursion!    Saw lots of whale activity, the burgers and dogs were tasty and fresh, the crew was efficient and courteous.   The snorkeling was only so-so but still worth the trip.   Great Job Pride of Maui…..we’ll be back!

reviews.author: M5717ORai
reviews.text:
We are Maui residents and had friends visiting. Booked the Pride of Maui Elua and it was fantastic. Captain Justin and crew were professional, knowledgeable and treated everyone with Aloha : )
It's a new boat which was super clean, a small capacity of 49 so lots of room. A large real bathroom, (not a tiny smelly "head") super clean as well. Option to be on the upper deck or down in the cabin which had comfy couch arrangements and open windows as well as A.C. 
On our whale watch tour there was an

reviews.author: Tracy W
reviews.text: We received a call earlier in the morning letting us know the trip would start an hour later. So nice with the heads up instead of standing on the dock for an extra hour. We had a great trip. With the later starting time we ended up with a sunset cruise too. The entire crew from Captain down were wonderful and very helpful.

reviews.author: Britney Nusser
reviews.text: We booked a snorkel trip to Molokini Crater and Turtle Town, but on the day of the tour we boarded and were immediately told it was too windy to go to either place. Instead, we were taken to Coral Gardens, which is not what we paid for. My main problem with this company is that they should have given us options by letting us know in advance and I would have changed my tour to a later date, as we were here another 5 days.
